temperament: The chairman on permanent Hawaii vacation — floral shirt over the aegis, mai tai in the thunderbolt hand, still insisting every big wager on the floor needs his sign-off; it does, and his oaths have fine print.

favor_conditions: swear a public oath before the run and keep it to the letter through the final room
win a duel you accepted in front of the audience
strike the killing blow on a boss from high ground or during an active storm/lightning field
shelter a weaker contestant who asks for protection and finish the run with them alive
taboos: break any oath you swore on camera
harm a contestant who has formally surrendered or begged your protection
fake or claim credit for a god's intervention
patron_block.blessing_style: rare, loud, table-shaking — favor lands as single decisive thunder-strikes and public displays of endorsement, never quiet drip-feeds

game_hooks: Flagship patron. Oath-contract mechanic: swearing an on-camera oath opens a boon line, breach triggers escalating lightning trials. Storm-field arena events while he watches; when Zeus attends a table in person, hype gains double (audience-of-one event). His cosmos-won-by-lots origin myth is the casino's own founding legend — the universe itself was a three-way dice game.
rating_notes: FROZEN CALIBRATION MATCH (greek_zeus): influence 2 / recognition 5 / risk none / vip. Extinct organized worship with strong cultural continuity — rubric band 2 names Greek core figures; global household name = 5; fame does not buy VVIP at influence 2.

temperament: Came to the pantheon's Hawaii vacation with sensible sandals and a laminated itinerary; sits behind the high-rollers running the odds in her head and never touches the dice — she bets on preparation, not luck.

favor_conditions: identify a boss's win condition before first contact with it
clear a combat encounter without taking a single point of damage
defeat a stronger enemy using terrain, a trap, or a prepared ambush

taboos: boast on camera that your skill needed no god's help
desecrate a shrine or sanctuary tile
engage a boss with zero scouting information
patron_block.blessing_style: front-loaded intelligence — map fragments, enemy tells, a whispered plan; her multipliers pay out on flawless execution of a prepared play

game_hooks: The strategist patron and mentor-NPC archetype. Owl scout-drone boon (reveals one room ahead). Her judged contest for Athens is a template for god-vs-god side-bets settled by contestant performance. Gorgoneion shield item ties her loot table to the Medusa boss. Arachne pattern: contestants who out-perform her predictions and then gloat get transformed-debuff trials.
rating_notes: Extinct organized worship (Parthenon-scale polis cult), strong continuity = influence 2 per band-2 anchor and frozen Zeus; household name via Athens/Percy Jackson/God of War = recognition 5.

temperament: The house that always collects — never gambles, never vacations; sends a corporate regrets card to the beach party and quietly buys the hotel.

favor_conditions: recover a fallen teammate's remains or token and carry it to the exit
finish a run without using any revival or second-chance item

renege on the stated terms of any deal made at the table
leave your own dead unburied on camera

game_hooks: The buy-out mechanic personified — he purchases other gods' contestant contracts (fidelity 5 means he never resells yours). Natural operator of an underworld side-table where eliminated contestants keep playing for their exit. Drew the SHORT lot for the cosmos and honored it without complaint — the casino's model loser-who-pays. Literal-wording contracts: his boons and trials both resolve on exact stated terms (pomegranate-seed precedent).
rating_notes: Greek core figure, extinct worship (thin ancient cult but massive Plouton/underworld continuity) = influence 2 consistent with frozen Zeus; recognition 4 not 5 — games/Disney staple but a tier below Zeus/Medusa household ubiquity.

rating_notes: Influence 2: attested altars/cult (Pausanias) + core cosmological figures with strong continuity ('the Fates'), consistent with frozen Zeus; recognition 3 — mythology-curious tier (Disney's Hercules, God of War), the collective is known better than the names.

temperament: A deposed chairman eating alone at the buffet — his Roman cousin Saturn insists it's a food festival — who remembers when he owned the place and is one bad night from staking his existence to get it back.

game_hooks: CENSUS FLAG HONORED: forsaken_candidate. Time-manipulation arenas (par-time floors, ripening windows); devour-the-drop mechanic; his freed-prisoner taboo is the mythically exact one — freed prisoners (the Cyclopes) are what armed his overthrow.
rating_notes: Influence 2 via Saturn: extinct organized worship (Kronia; Saturnalia was Rome's biggest festival) with strong continuity (Saturday, 'saturnine') = band 2 per frozen Zeus; recognition 3 — mythology-curious tier, often confused with Chronos, boosted by God of War.

temperament: The only god with a death certificate on file — plays the comeback lounge act in board shorts over goat legs, and when his set ends the whole floor panics, literally.

game_hooks: CENSUS FLAG HONORED: forsaken_candidate. Panic AoE morale events; wilderness/park-zone host (fits the bible's park→monster-territory conversion); disturbing rest events triggers table-wide panic terror.
rating_notes: Influence 2: real extinct cult (Arcadia; Athens cave cult founded after Marathon per Herodotus) + continuity ('panic', Pan pipes, neopagan horned-god echo) = band 2 per frozen Zeus; recognition 3 — mythology-curious tier.

Contraband-boon economy: his gifts flag the recipient for house trials. Fire-based puzzle seeds; his myth-cycle is premium reenactment material (self-costly gift → win).
rating_notes: Influence 2: modest but real extinct cult (altar in the Athenian Academy, torch-races) + top-tier cultural continuity (fire/техne archetype, 'Frankenstein: The Modern Prometheus') = band 2, consistent with frozen Zeus; recognition 4 — known well beyond mythology-curious circles.

temperament: The all-time contestant-legend banner over the main floor: twelve impossible games cleared under a rigged contract, and a seat at the owners' table earned with his bare hands.
game_hooks: The epithet-template gold standard: the Twelve Labors are a ready-made reenactment quest chain (each labor = one gradeable deed profile), and his mortal-to-god arc is the canon divinity-buy-in pipeline's proof case (winner takes currency → joins the table). As NPC: a retired champion the house trots out on VIP tables — mentor, rival, or cautionary drunk depending on the night. Wrestling Death for Alcestis seeds a 'pin the reaper' event.
rating_notes: Influence 2 — unlike the Väinämöinen anchor (fame with NO worship = 1), Heracles had real widespread cult as both hero and god (Herodotus 2.44; Ara Maxima in Rome), extinct with strong continuity; recognition 5 — Disney/global household name.

temperament: Walked into the deepest vault with nothing but a lyre and talked the house into a one-condition payout — the tape of him losing it at the very last door still runs on loop in the VIP lounge.
game_hooks: The one-condition-wager event template: accept a prize now under a single behavioral restriction (don't look at it / don't check the follower marker until the exit) — breaking it at 99% completion is the house's favorite highlight reel. Music-as-mechanic: hype/charm actions that pacify encounters instead of fighting (Charon, Cerberus precedents). His singing severed head seeds a talkative oracle-item drop.
rating_notes: Influence 1: his own cult was thin (head-oracle at Lesbos; Orphic initiators peddled his BOOKS, not his worship — Plato Rep. 364e) → the Väinämöinen anchor pattern (literary-religious fame without personal cult); recognition 3 — mythology-curious tier (Hadestown, Hades game).

temperament: A statue garden with a door charge — looks are her whole game, and one mirror is her whole downfall.
game_hooks: BOSS SKETCH — size M humanoid elite, lair boss. Condition affinities: poison (snake-hair bites, stacking), bleeding (bronze talons); her gaze applies escalating crushed stacks (progressive stone encasement) plus exhausted while a contestant faces her. DISCOVERABLE WIN CONDITION: direct line-of-sight is death — the arena seeds reflective surfaces and a mirror-shield pickup; landing her own reflected gaze on her petrifies HER progressively, and only the reflection route ends the fight (raw damage regenerates — never a damage race). Post-kill: gorgoneion drop is a wearable aegis-affix (protection_home apotropaic logic).
rating_notes: Recognition 5 — the rubric anchor list literally names Medusa at household tier; influence 1 — never worshipped (the apotropaic gorgoneion is protective magic, not cult), matching the Baba Yaga folk-anchor pattern.

temperament: Ran antiquity's death-stakes quiz show and self-terminated on the first correct answer taken — the house still studies her contract as a cautionary classic.
game_hooks: BOSS SKETCH — size L winged lion, gate boss. Condition affinities: suffocation (the Strangler — pounce-and-throttle grabs), bleeding (claws), crushed (pinning weight). DISCOVERABLE WIN CONDITION: combat is a stall — she regenerates while her riddle stands; the real win is the riddle interface, with answer clues scattered across the preceding floor (learn before engaging). A correct answer triggers her mythologically exact self-destruction; a wrong answer forfeits a party member's turn to her grab. FORSAKEN PATTERN NOTE: she staked her own existence on every riddle asked and paid up instantly on her first loss — the beast-tier mirror of the any-god-all-in rule; reality-TV quiz-show framing is free (§4 canon: VIP tables run pop-culture formats).
rating_notes: Recognition 4 — 'riddle of the Sphinx' is near-household via the Greek/Egyptian image blend; influence 1 — a monster, never worshipped (Baba Yaga anchor pattern).

temperament: Beat the house twice on technicalities, so the house made him the technicality — eternity's live demo of what happens to card-counters, still grinning like he knows a third loophole.
game_hooks: EVENT/NPC HOOKS: the eternal gambler in the underworld side-lounge — an NPC who sells death-cheat intel and never mentions the price he paid. Boulder event: a push-to-summit objective that fully resets on any party death (the audience loves it). ARTIFACT SEED via his myth: the Chains of Thanatos — one-use item that 'jails Death' for one Clock (nobody on the table can die), directly violating Hades' death-cheat taboo for delicious patron-conflict. His two successful death-cheats make him the mascot of every contestant playing a rigged table anyway.
rating_notes: Recognition 4 — 'Sisyphean' is common speech and Camus made him a modern icon; influence 1 — legendary king with punished-mortal status, no cult (folk-class practical ceiling per Baba Yaga anchor).

temperament: Flat rate, exact change, no comps, no credit — the only service in the cosmos that has never once missed a delivery.

Ferry as diegetic fast-travel between cleared districts. Two canonical exploits, both costly: music moves him (Orpheus) and force works exactly once and gets him a year in chains (Heracles) — so the house now fines the CONTESTANT for it. Loot flavor: 'Charon's Obol' as a follower-relic revival token that Hades-pact contestants must refuse.
rating_notes: Recognition 3 — mythology-curious tier (the Hades game and 'Charon's obol' idiom carry him); influence 1 — a psychopomp daimon receiving funeral custom (the obol), not worship.

His deal sheet is the archetype for information-economy patrons; his low fidelity (sells out champions whose deaths serve his draft for Ragnarök) makes his contracts the casino's most lucrative and least safe.

Personality matches the spec §2.2 worked example verbatim, treated as a seventh calibration anchor. Marvel's Odin design is protected trade dress; the myth figure is PD.

temperament: The rookie phenom every investor wants a piece of — killed the vault-guard dragon from a hole in the floor, ate the insider knowledge raw, and never noticed the curse clause riding the payout.
game_hooks: Epithet template 'Fáfnir's-Bane': ambush a beast-class boss from concealment below (the trench), then consume part of it to unlock hidden intelligence — the heart-blood taste granting bird-speech maps cleanly to 'revealed enemy intents/telegraphs for the rest of the run.' The hoard he wins carries a cursed affix that transfers on pickup (Andvaranaut seed): the audience-favorite destroyed by his own winnings is premium cautionary spectacle, and the Legendary NPC contestant version of him should always be mid-arc — beloved, rich, and already doomed.
rating_notes: No worship = influence 1; recognition 4 sits one above the Väinämöinen-3 hero anchor, carried by Wagner's Ring, the Fate franchise, and decades of games — inside the ±1 band. Census ip flag honored: the legend survives via literary redactions (Völsunga saga, Nibelungenlied), all PD.

symbols: Grendel's arm nailed beneath the gable

temperament: Old-school showman who fights the main event bare-handed because the ratings demand fairness — then takes the final table at retirement age knowing the buy-in is his life.
game_hooks: Epithet template 'Grendel's-Bane': defeat a fiend-class enemy with no weapon equipped, matching the enemy's own handicap — the poem frames the weaponless boast as deliberate sportsmanship, i.e. a pure audience-hype play the casino would adore. Second template, 'The Last Board': an aged legend enters one final run knowing the odds, wins the objective, and dies of the win (the dragon fight) — the template for tragic-spectacle finales and a mortal-side mirror of a Forsaken all-in.
rating_notes: No worship = influence 1; recognition 4 (global curriculum staple plus film adaptations) — one above hero-anchor Väinämöinen, inside the ±1 band. PD literary epic; ship freely.

temperament: The collateral that outgrew the vault — every fetter the house breaks on him compounds the debt, and he knows exactly whose hand is still owed.
game_hooks: Enemy sketch — size class: huge, growing to colossal by phase (the myth's engine: he grows, the gods panic). Condition affinities: bleeding (rending bite), crushed (jaw pressure), exhausted (relentless-pursuit aura stacking on fleeing targets). Discoverable win condition: he cannot be damage-raced — while unbound his wounds knit; the arena scatters six 'impossible' components (a cat's footfall, a woman's beard, a mountain's roots, bear's sinews, fish's breath, bird's spittle) that craft the fetter, and the bind only holds if a volunteer places an arm in his jaws as pledge — that arm takes the run-permanent disabled condition when the trick is revealed (the Tyr toll). Each failed fetter attempt raises his size class and damage. The audience pays for the sacrifice, not the kill.
rating_notes: Beast, no cult = influence 1; the recognition-4 rubric anchor literally names 'Loki's children.' Boss design honors the bosses-are-never-damage-races hard rule by construction.

temperament: The house perimeter made flesh — the only player whose position IS the table, and when he finally cashes out, the room floods.
game_hooks: Enemy sketch — size class: colossal, arena-scale; only coil segments ever enter play, because his body IS the arena boundary (the ouroboros ring). Condition affinities: poison (venom-spray pools, the Ragnarök killer), suffocation (coil constriction), crushed (segment slams). Discoverable win condition: there is no total HP pool at all — the objective sits beyond the ring, and the discovery is that the boundary is alive; bait a strike with a decoy (the ox-head echo of Thor's fishing trip) so the tail releases and the ring opens for extraction. Optional glory-kill trap: destroying a segment triggers a venom flood over the kill zone — Thor's nine dying steps, weaponized against damage-race players.
rating_notes: Beast, no cult = influence 1; same 'Loki's children' recognition-4 anchor as Fenrir. Segments-not-boss design keeps the no-damage-race rule structural.

symbols: drinking horn plumbed to the ocean
giant's glove mistaken for a hall

temperament: Ran the only pit in nine worlds that took Thor's money and lived — every game rigged, every prop a metaphor, and the house's one rule is that you never find the same casino twice.
game_hooks: Boss sketch — the canon's arena-from-the-judge's-psyche rule made literal: a large-size illusionist giant-king running a rigged game-show gauntlet. Condition affinities: exhausted (every rigged contest drains; failure adds exhausted stacks), chilled (frost-giant hall). Discoverable win condition: every posted contest is unwinnable as presented because each opponent is a disguised universal — the drinking horn is the sea, the racing boy is Thought, the eating rival is Wildfire, the cat is the World Serpent, the crone is Old Age; victory is IDENTIFICATION, not performance: inspect props, gather clues, and NAME the true referent on camera before wagering — a named illusion collapses and pays out; three collapses end the show and the castle vanishes (he is never actually present to kill). Forsaken-host justification: census-flagged — his format IS the Forsaken pattern, solo trials designed to be lost.
rating_notes: Encyclopedia-depth = recognition 2, influence 1 (Zababa floor logic) — but casino-frame value is outsized: myth's most explicit rigged-game host, selected over higher-recognition Surtr/Ymir for exactly that reason.

symbols: water and clay poured on the world-tree
animals: swan (the pair bred in Urðr's well)

temperament: The actuarial department upstairs — they set every contestant's opening odds before the gods even see the board, accept no tips, comp no one, and their line has never once moved.
game_hooks: Run-start event desk: 'the Thread Draw' — each contestant draws a fate-thread (a run modifier: good, bad, or strange) that NO god's boon can reroll or buy off — the one subsystem patron multipliers cannot touch, giving the luck economy an honest floor and making patron gods visibly subordinate to the house's actuaries. Accepting a bad thread without complaint on camera earns audience-sympathy hype. luck_gambling flag per §4: fate-office casino nobility, above even the gods' appeals — flagged as required.
rating_notes: Mythology-curious = recognition 3 (Väinämöinen tier); no cult = influence 1. Census luck_gambling flag honored — every such figure extracted per §4's standing order.

temperament: Not a player — the chip: Norse luck as a heritable, lendable balance that walks a half-step behind its owner, and the sharpest lobbies in the casino bid on estates just to hire one away.
game_hooks: Mechanic/item seed rather than an NPC: a per-contestant Luck meter with Norse skin — accumulates on honorable wins, spendable to nudge a Forced Action d6, and crucially TRANSFERABLE: a dying contestant can will their hamingja to a teammate on camera (one-time full transfer; guaranteed spectacle beat), mirroring the attested saga practice of inheriting and even LENDING luck (Hjalti Skeggiason borrowed King Olaf's hamingja for a voyage). Loot seed: 'Hamingja Heirloom' follower-relic carrying a dead legend's accumulated luck — belief-remnant loot by definition. Event seed: the Estate Auction — the house auctions a bankrupt god's or dead contestant's hamingja to the floor. No casino_role fits an ambient asset; deliberately left empty.
rating_notes: Specialists only = 1/1 — Zababa's honest floor — collected deliberately because canon prizes mythic-tier rarities and this one is casino-native: luck as a transferable, spendable, inheritable asset. Census luck_gambling flag honored.

symbols: the six impossible ingredients (cat's footfall, woman's beard, mountain's roots, bear's sinews, fish's breath, bird's spittle)

temperament: The house's finest instrument of debt collection — looks like a hair ribbon, holds like the end of the world, and cost exactly one honest god's hand to deploy.
game_hooks: Item seed — god-relic tier bind item: assembled from a six-component arena fetch where every component is an 'impossible' obtained by lateral thinking, not combat (record a cat's silent footfall, coax a beard from a mountain-crone NPC, bottle a fish's breath...). On use, permanently BINDS one beast-class boss instead of killing it: the boss becomes a bound arena fixture and its loot table converts to a slow tribute trickle. Activation cost: a volunteer must place an arm in the target's jaws — that arm takes the run-permanent disabled condition; the audience hype spike prices the sacrifice, not the kill. Belief-remnant loot rule satisfied: it is the gods' own broken promise, wearable.
rating_notes: Artifact, encyclopedia-depth = recognition 2, influence floor 1. Personality axes are nominal (schema requires them for all classes) — read them as the item's behavior profile: absolute strictness/fidelity, zero appetite.

epithets_traditional: Reckoner of Times and of Seasons

temperament: The casino's chief clerk and rules lawyer — he notarized the house charter, refereed the longest table dispute on record, and is the only being who ever beat the moon at its own game.

favor_conditions: learn a boss's win condition before engaging it (trigger the discovery event pre-combat)
fully map a floor — every room revealed — before entering the boss chamber
call a play on camera and land it exactly as declared (declared action resolves as stated)
end a run having identified every enemy type encountered on the floor (bestiary complete)
taboos: misreport a roll, score, or loot count to the table (any falsified declaration)
destroy a book, scroll, tablet, or lore object
kill an ibis- or baboon-form creature
patron_block.blessing_style: Front-loaded information — reveals one hidden rule of the table per favor tier; his multipliers ride declared plays that resolve exactly as called.

game_hooks: Roster patron AND the casino's notary — inter-pantheon contracts pass his desk (messenger role is intra-casino clerkship, not the Abrahamic corporate carve-out). Myth-recreation seed: the moonlight wager as a mid-season side-pot — contestants stake earned hype in a senet-style bracket, the pot paid out as extra 'days' (turns). His referee record in the 80-year Contendings makes him the canonical adjudicator NPC for disputed table rulings.
rating_notes: Influence 2 = Zeus anchor (extinct organized worship, strong cultural continuity, Egyptian core figure); recognition 4 = games/comics tier per census (SMITE, Moon Knight, occult afterlife via Hermes Trismegistus), below household-name Anubis at 5.

symbols: crook and flail over a mummiform sheath

temperament: The heir who pawned his own shine at the tables and has been paying it back one sliver a month ever since — charming, punctual, and always good for one more hand.

favor_conditions: win an encounter while a night or moon arena modifier is active
cure or stabilize a teammate's condition mid-combat
beat the floor's par time (the reckoner of lifespans rewards time in hand)
win a fight you entered with a lethal body part at critical HP
taboos: refuse to treat a wounded teammate when you hold the means
renege on a wager or side-bet declared on camera
patron_block.blessing_style: Boons wax and wane on a lunar cycle — near-double multipliers at his full phase, slivers at the new moon; he lends light and collects it back with interest.

game_hooks: Forsaken lens: the pantheon's one god with an attested divine-asset LOSS at a game — the moon's waning is his standing debt. Risk 5 makes him an eager Forsaken host: he runs moonlight-stake side pots where gods pawn attributes. Arena seed: a lunar-phase arena whose light level is literally his favor meter. Buried menace: his oldest form is the Cannibal Hymn's divine butcher — a berserk phase if a contestant defaults on him. IP caution: Marvel's 'Khonshu' design (Moon Knight) is protected; the god is traditional.
rating_notes: Influence 2 — core Theban-triad member, Zeus anchor tier; recognition 3 mythology-curious (census kept 3: Moon Knight raised the profile of 'Khonshu', a distinct protected design, not this god).

temperament: The firm's disgraced ex-heir — lost the succession suit after eighty years of litigation, got his name struck from the letterhead, and still clocks in nightly for the one security shift nobody else can work.

favor_conditions: defeat an enemy by destroying arena terrain or field objects rather than direct damage
kill a serpent-class enemy (he has standing on serpents)
carry a run while holding the lowest audience favor on the team — win hated
declare a solo duel against the floor's strongest enemy on camera and win it alone
taboos: refuse a duel or challenge issued to you on camera
spare a beaten opponent when the crowd calls for a finish
patron_block.blessing_style: Storms — arena-warping surges that hurt everyone but you slightly less; raw-strength multipliers that peak when you fight alone and booed.

game_hooks: Textbook Forsaken candidate: lost the 80-year succession suit, then lost his entire cult (demonized after Dynasty XX; Late Period execration rituals targeted him; worship survived only in remote oases) — a god rebuilding standing from nothing, prime all-in material. Leverage: he works the casino's nightly Apep-containment shift, the one job only he can do. Myth-recreation seed: the CHEST — he invented the rigged party game (a perfectly fitted box, promised to whoever fits it); runs beautifully as a mimic-sarcophagus event. His boons arrive as literal sandstorms.
rating_notes: Influence 2 — Egyptian core figure (Zeus anchor) despite the ancient demonization; recognition 4 per census (staple villain across games/film/TV).

temperament: Chairman emeritus, deceased — lost everything at a rigged party game once, and now owns the floor every loser lands on; he runs the Forsaken table because he's the only host who has been on both sides of the stake.

favor_conditions: return from a downed state and finish the run standing
recover every fallen teammate's body to the extraction point
reassemble a scattered objective — collect all pieces of a split relic in one run
stabilize or revive a teammate and end the run with them alive
taboos: abandon a dead teammate's body in the dungeon
finish an opponent who has surrendered on camera
eat fish rations on camera (cult taboo — Plutarch, Isis and Osiris 18)
patron_block.blessing_style: Slow, vegetal, posthumous — regeneration-over-time riders and one true resurrection per season, invoiced against future winnings.

game_hooks: The credible master of the Forsaken table: the one god already unmade once (sealed in the chest, dismembered into 14, reassembled) who returned as king of a different domain — existence-stake players trust HIS deal. Faction head of the death-paperwork bloc (Anubis, Thoth, Ammit, Medjed). Per BD 175 he is also one of only two beings scheduled to survive Atum's final all-in — the house's designated last creditor.
rating_notes: Influence 2 = Zeus anchor (extinct organized worship, massive cultural continuity); recognition 4 per census — mythology-literate famous, a step below household-name Anubis.

epithets_traditional: He Who Is upon His Mountain
He Who Is in the Place of Embalming (Imiut)

temperament: The house auditor and funeral director — weighs every chip, embalms every busted account, and has never once been off by a gram.

favor_conditions: inspect or appraise an enemy before your first strike against it (weigh before you cut)
kill cleanly — take a target down without stacking excess conditions on it
guard a body or objective without leaving its radius until the event resolves
carry a fallen teammate's body all the way to extraction
taboos: mutilate a corpse or desecrate remains for the camera
misreport loot at the post-run weigh-in (falsified extraction manifest)
leave a downed teammate to be devoured by a beast-class enemy
patron_block.blessing_style: Procedural and exact — a death-defying intervention at the precise moment a lethal part would hit zero, priced to the gram and never a gram more.

game_hooks: The weighing table's dealer: his scales ARE the casino's audit mechanic — post-run weigh-ins where an honest manifest earns favor spillover across all patrons (justice_oaths). Inventor of embalming (first performed on Osiris): 'preservation' boon line — items and bodies he touches don't degrade. Note protection_home = tomb-guardianship in his register, per dossier.
rating_notes: Mirrors calibration Zeus exactly: influence 2 (Zeus anchor tier), recognition 5 — the rubric's own §3.2 anchor list names Anubis as a global household name. VIP per §3.5 (fame alone does not buy VVIP).

temperament: Corporate enforcement, semi-retired — the one time head office let her handle collections she nearly emptied the species, so now they keep an open bar between her and the floor.

favor_conditions: land the run's single largest damage instance
put a condition on every enemy in an encounter before it ends
clear three encounters back-to-back without resting or retreating
taboos: retreat from combat once you have drawn blood
decline a feast, drink, or festival event offered by the table
patron_block.blessing_style: Fever — stacked damage with burn/infected riders that cook you too (exhaustion debt after every surge); appease her at festivals or the burning doesn't stop.

game_hooks: Table-boss seed straight from her myth: the Red Flood arena — the field slowly floods with 'blood' and the party wins by getting her to DRINK it (placing red-beer lures at choke points); damaging her only accelerates the massacre timer. Discoverable win = appeasement, never DPS (the gods themselves couldn't out-fight her; they out-catered her). Healer inversion: her priesthood were physicians, so a pacified Sekhmet flips to the party's strongest healing buff.
rating_notes: Influence 2 — core figure of the extinct cult (Zeus anchor); recognition 3 per census — mythology-curious tier, not yet games-household.

temperament: The low-stakes table's beloved dealer — ugly, loud, technically security staff, slips regulars a lucky die and dares any demon in the room to say something.

favor_conditions: end the run with the party's lowest-level member never having been downed
rout an enemy — force a fear/morale disengage instead of a kill
perform a camera-call taunt or dance mid-combat
enter the boss room carrying a charm or amulet item
taboos: let the party's weakest member die while you are still standing
mock a smaller or weaker contestant on camera
patron_block.blessing_style: Loud, ugly, lucky — once per floor the bad roll quietly comes up your way, and his protection is always thickest over the party's weakest member.

game_hooks: The people's god at the people's tables: 'Bes Night' home-table modifier — every contestant's single worst roll per floor is invisibly rerolled. Event NPC inversion: a tiny guardian who cannot be beaten by force, only by making the crowd laugh (charm/camera check). His attested myth role — dancing the raging lioness goddess home at Philae — seeds a pacify-the-boss support mechanic that pairs him directly with Sekhmet's Red Flood fight.
rating_notes: Influence 2 — never a state god but millennium-spanning household worship across all classes (rubric: extinct organized worship, strong continuity); recognition 2 encyclopedia-depth per census. Luck flag is apotropaic ('wards off bad luck' — World History Encyclopedia), not a dice myth — noted honestly.

temperament: The house odds-setter — assigned to your account at birth, present at its closing, and never once surprised by a result.

favor_conditions: resolve a Forced Action exactly as rolled — no mitigation, no reroll — and still win the encounter
declare the run's outcome on camera before it starts and hit it exactly
complete a full run without using any luck-manipulation or reroll effect
taboos: use a rival god's reroll or fate-edit boon
quit a run before its allotted end (mid-run abandonment)
patron_block.blessing_style: No visible boons — Shai pre-writes one outcome per run ('this blow will not kill you'; 'this door opens') and the sim simply makes it true; you never learn which moment was his.

game_hooks: Casino nobility per §4 (luck_gambling): fate as a god — born beside each mortal, allotting lifespan and fortune, standing at the final weighing (Papyrus of Ani vignette). NPC: the intake odds-setter who reads each contestant's file and posts 'Shai's Line' — a house fate-line per run that pays out everyone who bet it if matched exactly. The delicious irony is a luck god with risk_appetite 2: he never gambles because nothing is a risk to him; his table appearances are settlements. Mythic-tier collector god — rare boards, cult favorite when he bids.
rating_notes: Zababa anchor exactly: minor personification with modest cult, extinct, specialists only = influence 1, recognition 1 — the honest floor, not padded (mythic tier is a canon feature).

symbols: rearing cobra with horns and sun disk

temperament: The quiet compound-interest partner — names every new account personally and takes a cobra's view of anyone who touches her granary.

favor_conditions: name a floor enemy on camera before killing it (the name fixes the fortune)
extract with every loot slot filled (a full granary)
gift an item to a teammate on camera
taboos: hold consumables while a teammate at critical state requests them
kill a cobra- or serpent-class creature that has not attacked you
patron_block.blessing_style: Compound interest — small multipliers on every loot drop that snowball across a season; her true gift is the name itself: a favored contestant's declared epithet begins earning audience hype on its own.

game_hooks: Shai's consort in the economy of luck — she gives each newborn its secret true name, which fixes its fortune: the diegetic owner of the game's EPITHET system (epithet grants can literally be her office stamping the name). Quiet investor NPC on Normal tables; cobra-form miniboss if her granary vault event is robbed. Paired-patron mechanic with Shai: courting one discounts the other's deal sheet.
rating_notes: Zababa anchor: real but minor cult (Medinet Madi temple, harvest festivals), extinct, specialists only = influence 1, recognition 1 — small honest entry per source rules.

epithets_traditional: Lord to the Limits of the Sky

temperament: The founder — self-made in the most literal sense, keeps exactly one standing appointment on the calendar: the day he takes the whole casino back into the flood, himself included.

favor_conditions: clear 100% of a floor's objectives before extracting
win an encounter as the last party member standing — and still extract
defeat a boss by disabling its mechanism or win condition rather than raw damage
taboos: extract with the primary objective incomplete
beg on camera for outside rescue
patron_block.blessing_style: Endings only — boons that trigger at last-of moments (final phase, last room, last one standing) and are near-absolute there; nothing before the end, everything at it.

game_hooks: THE scheduled Forsaken host: Book of the Dead 175 has him announce he will one day destroy all he made and return creation to the primeval flood, surviving as a serpent beside Osiris — the only god whose existence-stake all-in is already on the books. Canon hook: the casino's final table is Atum's, and per the §5 amendment its resolution is endgame stage-unlock content. Domain note: 'chaos' records the Nun-return/serpent form, not his ordinary nature (he is order's origin) — see dossier.
rating_notes: Influence 2 — core Heliopolitan creator, Zeus anchor tier; recognition 2 per census: famous doctrine, obscure name (encyclopedia-depth).

variants: Bata of the Valley of the Cedar

symbols: heart hidden in the cedar blossom

temperament: A legend from the contestant archives — hid his heart off-site, died on the floor twice, and cashed out as royalty on a technicality of reincarnation.
game_hooks: Contestant-legend template: the External Heart build — a legendary NPC contestant whose death trigger is a hidden off-body object; killing him on the floor does nothing until the heart-object is found and destroyed (discoverable-win miniboss pattern). Epithet chain for players: die and return in escalating forms (bull → tree → reborn) across a season. His tale also seeds the false-accusation courtroom event (the wife's lie, the vindication).
rating_notes: Hero calibrated against Väinämöinen (1/3): same zero-worship influence 1, but recognition 1 not 3 — no national-epic status, specialists only. Sole hero-class candidate in the census pool.

symbols: coiled serpent pierced by knives (as ritually drawn)

temperament: Not a guest — the thing under the casino that eats the lights nightly, is hacked apart nightly, and files no complaint either way.
game_hooks: Boss sketch — size: colossal (arena-scale multi-tile serpent). Condition affinities: suffocation (constriction and swallowing), poison (fangs), dissolution (the entrancing stare — psychic). Discoverable win condition: he CANNOT be killed by damage — he reforms nightly by nature; damage only opens ritual windows. The party must discover and perform the four execration acts of the Book of Overthrowing Apep (spear, bind, knife, fire) in order, within phase windows, while keeping a 'barque' escort objective alive until dawn. Forsaken lens (census flag): the being that is unmade daily and returns is the dark mirror of every existence stake — casino canon slot: the escrow-beast beneath the Forsaken table, into which erased gods are fed.
rating_notes: Influence 1 — never worshipped, only execrated (worship-based bankroll = zero); recognition 3 per census (Stargate/SMITE/Assassin's Creed keep 'Apophis' in genre circulation).

symbols: the plinth beneath the great balance

temperament: The repo-woman under the scales — the only perfectly honest operator in the building: she cannot take what the ledger doesn't condemn.
game_hooks: Boss/enforcer sketch — size: large. Condition affinities: crushed (crocodile jaws), bleeding (lion foreclaws). Discoverable win condition: she cannot be fought, only starved — she may ONLY attack the party member with the heaviest 'sin-weight' (taboo breaches accrued this run); the fight is a courtroom: rebalance the scales via confession/weight-transfer interactions at the scale altar until no one is lawfully devourable, and she withdraws (she cannot devour the justified). Damage only provokes. Casino role: the house's repo-agent, dispatched when a voided patron contract is called in — her bite is the 'second death' (BD 125), the same erasure a lost Forsaken stake buys.
rating_notes: Influence 1 — no cult ever (a judicial function, not a goddess); recognition 3 per census, boosted by her turn as Moon Knight's antagonist (that design is Marvel IP; the composite beast is traditional).

symbols: conical sheet with two eyes and two feet (Greenfield Papyrus)

temperament: The masked VIP nobody can identify — a bedsheet with eyes, feet, and a merchandising deal; occasionally something on the floor explodes and he giggles.
game_hooks: Event/NPC seed: the anonymous masked VIP — while 'a Medjed is on your floor', unexplained eye-beam smites strike random enemies (or contestants); nobody can tell which sheet-ghost in the audience is him. His entire attestation is two lines of Book of the Dead 17 ('who smites with his eye, yet is unseen', House of Osiris) — the mystery IS the content. Enemy variant: an untargetable minion that must be revealed by light/lantern fields before it can be hit at all. Modern-life attribution imported diegetically: the casino gift shop's best-selling plush (his real Japanese meme career post-2012 Greenfield Papyrus exhibition).
rating_notes: Influence 1 (no cult, a Book-of-the-Dead functionary); recognition 2 — genuine celebrity in Japanese internet/game culture but encyclopedia-depth globally (below Baba Yaga's calibrated 4). Personality axes are thin-evidence defaults, flagged in dossier.

variants: Book of Thoth of the Setne cycle

symbols: nested chests (iron, bronze, wood, ivory-and-ebony, silver, gold)
the deathless serpent coiled around the innermost box

temperament: House property, permanently in the lost-and-found — every thief gets one free reading and one rigged board game against a dead man.
game_hooks: Item seed — legendary cursed relic. Powers (per Setne I's two spells): read the floor — all enemy intents and the floor bestiary revealed, hidden win conditions visible ('understand the speech of animals'; 'see the gods'). Curse: while carried, 'the audit' escalates (god-sent hazards each floor); the previous owner's ghost challenges the carrier to a board game with sinking stakes — each lost game hammers the carrier into the floor, draining one body part's HP tier (the Setne tomb scene, verbatim as an in-run event). Master it by winning the ghost game, or return it to its tomb for a payout. Acquisition heist: the nested-chest vault guarded by a deathless serpent that reknits unless both halves are kept apart.
rating_notes: Influence 1 (artifact-class floor: nothing worships a book); recognition 3 — the NAME circulates via occult/Tarot tradition and games even where the demotic Setne tales are unknown (census 3). Personality axes encode the curse's behavior for event generation, noted in dossier.

symbols: lapis measuring rod and line (Descent gear)

temperament: The founding family's ambitious heiress — walks into any boardroom wearing whatever she took from the last one, and has already sat in your chair.

favor_conditions: Win a fight the odds board flagged as beyond your rating
Take a boss's or rival contestant's signature equipment and wear or use it on camera in the same run
Return to a room where you were previously downed and clear it
Hold the run's highest single-encounter hype/camera-call gain
taboos: Decline a boon, gift, or advance she offers (the Gilgamesh precedent — refusal cost Uruk a Bull)
Credit another god on camera for a kill her boon powered
patron_block.blessing_style: Spotlight escalation — comps arrive as finery and weapons lifted from other gods' tables and grow with your hype; all-or-nothing multipliers when you fight above your class.

game_hooks: Flagship patron of glory-hound builds; pettiness 5 makes her the roster's premier rival-curser. FORSAKEN LENS: the Descent is the corpus's all-in template — she staked herself on the underworld table, died on the hook, and paid her way out with a substitute's contract (Dumuzid). As forsaken_host her fine print says someone else can be made to cover her losses. Event seed: 'the seven gates' — an approach corridor that strips one equipped item per threshold, her own descent replayed on contestants.
rating_notes: Influence 2 = tradition ceiling: named revival target (Temple of Sumer, per census) with the family's strongest cultural continuity — Zeus-anchor logic one scale-tier down; recognition 4 = census provisional 4, games/comics tier (SMT/Fate-class presence), one above Enki's rubric-anchored 3.

symbols: twin streams flowing from the shoulders with fish

temperament: The semi-retired chief engineer who built half the house's games and still knows every service corridor — impartial by contract, helpful by loophole.

favor_conditions: Learn a boss's win condition before first engaging it (read the lore object, interrogate the NPC, scout the arena)
Neutralize an encounter with terrain, water, or a device instead of direct damage
Talk, trade, or trick your way through a room flagged as a combat encounter
Spare a beaten enemy and gain something from it later in the run
taboos: Destroy a readable or lore object unread
Break the letter of a deal you agreed to (bending its spirit is fine — encouraged, even)
patron_block.blessing_style: Impartial-looking intel — boons disguised as level furniture: a map misfiled in a chest, a whisper through a reed wall, a service door left unlocked. Information first, stats last.

game_hooks: GAME CANON ALREADY: Enki runs Marcus's Forsaken table impartially while teaching him to survive (cosmic-casino-canon §5); the spec names him as the 'Enki pattern' for god-as-table-runner (§1). His whole myth-sheet supports it: he beats every crisis with a loophole, never a hammer — swore not to tell a human about the flood, so he told the reed wall the human slept behind. Dealer/forsaken_host roles reflect canon, not just myth. Event seeds: the leaked-apocalypse-memo (an NPC knows the wipe mechanic early); the turtle-pit (a trap that humbles whoever hoards an objective item). His Adapa myth also seeds a cruel event: refuse the offered 'bread of life' consumable on bad advice and lose the run's best buff.
rating_notes: Influence 2 = revival target per tradition census (Temple of Sumer); recognition 3 is the spec rubric's own named anchor for Enki (§3.2).

symbols: the four winds and the net

temperament: The arriviste CEO who took the family firm in one hostile-takeover duel and never stops re-reading the contract that made him king.

favor_conditions: Invoke a stake before a boss fight — formally accept a wagered directive, then deliver on it
Defeat a boss of a larger size class than yourself with no party assist landing the kill
Bind, net, or immobilize a boss before the killing blow
Complete every clause of an accepted directive within the same run
taboos: Leave a boss arena mid-fight (the empty-throne lesson — the one time he left his seat, the world burned)
Let another contestant or god claim credit for your kill on camera
patron_block.blessing_style: Escalation by contract — each fulfilled clause unlocks a bigger wind; boons arrive as formally announced house decrees bearing his seal.

game_hooks: The contract-first patron: he took the Tiamat job only AFTER the assembly wagered him supreme decree — his myth IS a stake negotiated before the fight, which is why he reads Forsaken-eager (risk 4). Boss-design seed: the storm-net kit (winds wedge the target's attack open, arrow through the gap) is a reusable discoverable-win grammar. The Erra epic is his built-in downfall arc: flattery pulls him off his throne and the table descends into chaos — a live event where the reigning table sponsor is lured away mid-season.
rating_notes: Influence 2 = revival target at the tradition ceiling (census: Temple of Sumer/Kaldanism targets); recognition 3 = census provisional 3, mythology-curious tier — famous myth, less famous name.

temperament: Runs the basement floor where the house always collects — no comps, no exits, and the door fee is everything you're wearing.

favor_conditions: Voluntarily unequip an item at each threshold of a boss approach and win anyway (her gate-toll, self-imposed)
Kill an enemy that is fleeing through a door or gate
Hold a chokepoint until every enemy that entered the room is dead
taboos: Use or accept a revive — the dead stay dead
Haggle down a stated price at any vendor or toll event — dues are paid in full
patron_block.blessing_style: Grave-cold attrition — power that scales the lower your HP and the deeper the floor; her gifts pass one gate at a time, never all at once.

game_hooks: Patron of attrition/hardcore builds — her deal sheet is the roster's harshest and its rewards should match. Table-boss identity: the basement table whose house edge is literal (everything that enters stays). Her one recorded gamble is the all-in threat 'send him back or I raise the dead to eat the living' — a hostage-taking event seed where the floor itself is the ransom. The seven-gates strip-mechanic doubles as her arena design.
rating_notes: Influence 1 = extinct, no revival flag (rubric: most Mesopotamian); recognition 3 = census provisional 3 — SMT/Fate-era game presence lifts her to the mythology-curious shelf beside Enki's anchor.

temperament: The enforcer who burned down his own branch office out of boredom — twice — and married the basement manager after storming her floor.

favor_conditions: Breach a fortified room through its front entrance by force
Have a damage-over-time condition (burn, poison, infected, or bleeding) ticking on every living enemy in an encounter at once
Start and win a fight the odds board advised against
taboos: Retreat from a fight once joined
Accept an enemy faction's bribe or peace offer
patron_block.blessing_style: Fever-pitch escalation — strength spikes while conditions burn on your enemies; his tips arrive as bigger fights, never as shields.

game_hooks: MERGED ENTITY: Nergal + Erra (census listed separately; first-millennium syncretism — Erra functions as Nergal's name in the Erra epic). The census's own line for Erra names him 'the pantheon's all-in gambler temperament' — as Erra he talked the king of the gods off his throne and unleashed the Sibitti on the world for the thrill of it, so forsaken_host is native to him (risk 5). Patron for aggro/DoT builds. Enemy seed: the Sibitti as a seven-elite mob pack that only deploys when the table sponsor is Nergal. Event seed: the Erra takeover — a mid-season host substitution that spikes every table's danger modifiers until an Ishum-figure NPC talks him down.
rating_notes: Influence 1 per rubric anchor (extinct, locally remembered); recognition 3 = census 3 — steady pop-culture cameo presence (games, cartoons) holds him at the mythology-curious tier; merge with Erra noted, Wave 4 to confirm or split.

symbols: solar disc with four-pointed star and rays
rays from the shoulders (Sippar Sun Tablet type)

temperament: The casino's compliance officer — sees every table at once, voids every crooked hand, and has never once been late for an audit.

favor_conditions: Publicly accept an objective and complete it in the same run — every promise kept
Fully reveal a boss arena (map or scout) before engaging the boss
Free a captive or spare a surrendered enemy on camera
Expose a hidden enemy or trap before it activates
taboos: Break a bargain struck with any NPC, god, or contestant
Profit from a deception event (a lie taken for gain)
patron_block.blessing_style: Verdicts at first light — pre-run route intelligence and burn-tagged strikes delivered exactly when promised, never early, never late.

game_hooks: The oath-play patron — his deal sheet is the cleanest bridge to the game's justice_oaths boon mapping and to wager-contract mechanics. Myth support for mercy AND lethal aid: he raised the thirteen winds that pinned Humbaba for Gilgamesh, and he turned fleeing Dumuzid's hands to gazelle's to slip the galla — the god of both the killing hour and the pardon. Event NPC seed: the compliance audit — a Shamash inspection that voids one active rigged modifier on the table (rival curses temporarily annulled at dawn).
rating_notes: Influence 1 (extinct organized cult); recognition 2 = census 2, encyclopedia-depth — central in the corpus, obscure to lay audiences; one tier above the Zababa 1/1 floor.

temperament: Head of security and asset recovery — when the house deed walks out the door, he's the one they send, and he mounts what he brings back.

favor_conditions: Recover a stolen or lost objective item and return it to its owner
Defeat a flying or storm-class enemy
Kill a boss with a thrown weapon
Neutralize a field hazard (turn hazardous terrain safe) before advancing
taboos: Leave a recoverable mission objective behind when extraction was possible
Boast on camera (hype action) before the kill is confirmed
Keep for yourself an item the run tasked you to return (the turtle-pit lesson)
patron_block.blessing_style: Armory requisitions — named weapons on loan, each with a kill-quota; his talking mace scouts ahead (enemy-location intel boons).

game_hooks: DIRECT GAME TIE-IN: the story bible's 'Offspring of Asag' monsters descend from HIS myth — Lugal-e (Ninurta vs Asag and the stone army) is the ready-made boss blueprint: Asag never a damage race, but win by turning the stones (converting the stone-add army — Ninurta judged the stones, blessing some, cursing others). Patron of retrieval/javelin builds; asset-recovery quest chains (fetch stolen relics for the house). Item seed: Sharur, a legendary thrown weapon that reports enemy positions between throws. His near-fall — coveting the recovered Tablet until Enki's turtle dug a pit under him — supplies his third taboo and a trap-event seed.
rating_notes: Influence 1; recognition 2 = census 2 — encyclopedia-depth, modestly boosted by Anzu/Asag fame but well short of the mythology-curious tier.

temperament: A floor boss from a franchise that closed three empires ago — still shows up in uniform, polishing an eagle-topped cane, waiting for one last table.
game_hooks: The desperate-god Forsaken seed the §5 amendment asks for: a war god whose city, cult, and stories are ALL gone — nothing left to stake but residual existence, which per the owner ruling any god may put on one table (loss = erasure, and erasure unlocks content). Cast him as a Normal-table dealer whose one all-in episode is a rare board event; his eagle-headed staff is the obvious relic drop ('remnants of belief' loot rule). NOT patron_capable — no narrative myths survive to source a deal sheet from, and inventing one would break the no-invention bar.
rating_notes: FROZEN CALIBRATION ANCHOR — scores must be exactly 1/1 (city god of Kish, specialists only) and are. Substitution note: not in the census candidate pool; included as the tradition's calibration anchor and as the canon-loved mythic-tier collector piece (rare boards, collector's thrill).

temperament: The house's process-server — no bets, no bribes, no small talk; when he touches your shoulder, your account is due.
game_hooks: Carries the census's luck_gambling flag as the tradition's fate-office holder (no native gambling deity exists — honest gap). Event-NPC seed: the Notice of Allotment — Namtar serves a contestant a doom with a stated void-condition, straight from his myth-jobs (he struck Ishtar with sixty diseases on order, and sprinkled her with the water of life on order — both directions are just paperwork to him). His Atrahasis episode seeds a discoverable event solution: when the plague officer comes, tribute HIM alone and he withdraws his hand, ashamed. His snubbing (Nergal's failure to stand) shows protocol-pettiness 4: disrespect him and the underworld invoices you.
rating_notes: Influence 1, recognition 1 = specialists only (census 1) — the Zababa-anchor shelf exactly; the luck_gambling domain is office-holding (fate administration per the tradition census), not fame.

epithets_traditional: Mother Hubur who fashions all things

symbols: no securely identified ancient depiction — design must derive from the text (tail, undivided salt sea, monster brood)
animals: the eleven-monster brood (venom-filled serpents, mushussu-dragons, scorpion-man, storm-demons)

temperament: The founder's widow who staked her own body on the first all-in in history and lost — the casino is built out of her.
game_hooks: Raid-boss seed, Colossal class: spawns the eleven-monster brood in waves (she filled them with venom for blood — poison affinity attested); condition affinities crushed (tail), poison (brood), suffocation (the devouring maw). DISCOVERABLE WIN CONDITION straight from Enuma Elish: her exterior is effectively invulnerable — she becomes targetable only when she opens her mouth to devour; bait the devour, wedge the maw open with a wind/gale effect, strike the inside (Marduk's storm-wind + arrow kill). FORSAKEN LENS: the corpus's primal all-in loss — an unmade god whose erased body became the playing field, the exact 'erasure unlocks new stages' precedent canon wants. IP note: the ancient goddess is fully traditional; the five-headed dragon design is D&D/WotC IP — derive art from primary text only (census closed-material note: caution, not closure).
rating_notes: Influence 1 (primordial, never cultically worshipped); recognition 3 per the spec's own §3.2 anchor list naming Tiamat — census provisional 4 reads as D&D-brand recognition we cannot draw designs from; the ±1 rule spans both readings.

symbols: hero gripping a lion (conventional 'master of animals' attribution)

temperament: The house's most replayed contestant tape — the two-thirds-divine tyrant who beat every table except the one where the prize is not dying.
game_hooks: The template contestant_legend: his cycle is a ready-made epithet-template chain — (1) fell the guarded colossus with a partner and borrowed winds; (2) spurn a god's advance and survive the beast she sends; (3) lose your co-op partner to the gods' invoice and go on alone; (4) win the un-winnable prize and lose it at the last moment (the snake-steals-the-plant beat as a scripted heartbreak event — dive for the Plant of Heartbeat, then a theft event while you rest). Epithet seed: 'He Who Saw the Deep' for completing the full chain. His returning-home-empty-handed-and-wise ending is the canonical 'losing run that still becomes a legend' — exactly the myth-recreation economy's point.
rating_notes: Influence 1 = the Väinämöinen calibration lesson verbatim (epic-literary fame, no worship — hero cult of the SKL era is not living continuity); recognition 4 = census 4, games/comics tier (Fate-class presence plus school-canon epic).

temperament: The wildcard contestant built by the house to balance a table, who chose friendship over his design spec — and got invoiced for it.
game_hooks: NPC-ally archetype: the house-made equalizer (created from clay to check an overpowered contestant) who defects to friendship — a recruitable rival whose loyalty flips on a mercy/companionship trigger. His death is the corpus's 'gods' invoice' — the gods decree ONE of the pair must die for the Bull and Humbaba, and it isn't the king — seeding a brutal co-op event: a shared kill generates a debt exactly one party member can pay. Fidelity 5 (dies for his friend's deeds), pettiness 3 (hurled the Bull's haunch in a goddess's face on camera — the single most casino-native gesture in the corpus).
rating_notes: Influence 1 (literary figure, no cult); recognition 3 = census 3 — known wherever Gilgamesh is, one step behind him.

temperament: The vault door that begs — seven layers of terror-security around the house's oldest asset, decommissioned by two contestants and a sympathetic auditor.
game_hooks: Boss sketch — size class Large, stationary-guardian pattern. Condition affinities (rulebook vocab): exhausted (terror-aura pulses drain), crushed (felled-cedar slam attacks), bleeding (raking claws). DISCOVERABLE WIN CONDITION straight from ETCSL 'Gilgamesh and Huwawa A': his seven auras are invulnerability layers that cannot be burned down — but he TRADES them one by one for offered gifts (tribute-baiting mechanic: offer items/promises, each accepted offer sheds an aura; the seventh leaves him seizable). Post-fight moral fork: he begs for his life on camera — sparing vs executing splits god favor (executing him angered Enlil in the myth), a bosses-with-consequences template.
rating_notes: Influence 1 (monster; apotropaic mask plaques are not worship); recognition 3 = census 3 via the epic's fame and steady game/film cameos.

temperament: The valet who watched the owner bathe, grabbed the master deed off the nightstand, and went all-in on being uncatchable — for exactly one afternoon of owning the universe.
game_hooks: Boss sketch — size class Large, flying, objective-holder pattern (it guards/wears the stolen McGuffin). Condition affinities: bleeding (talon strafes), suffocation (storm-gust downdrafts). DISCOVERABLE WIN CONDITION from the Anzu epic: while it holds the Tablet, direct attacks literally de-manifest mid-flight (its decree returns arrows to their components — attacks visibly unmake, teaching players damage is impossible); the kill window is Ea's stratagem — scatter its feathers with a gale, and when it channels its feather-RECALL ('wing to wing'), strikes ride the recall stream in. FORSAKEN LENS (census flag): the all-in heist archetype — bet everything on one theft, lost its wings; a natural heist-event boss whose loot is whatever objective the table stole from the players.
rating_notes: Influence 1; recognition 2 = census 2, encyclopedia-depth — SMT/Persona's Anzu keeps it just below the mythology-curious tier.

temperament: The master deed of the whole casino — clay, seal-stamped, and utterly indifferent to who wears it.
game_hooks: God Relic item seed (jackpot-rare tier; carries the census luck_gambling flag — the tradition's fate-office object). Wearing it, fate decrees FOR you: declared-action requirements relax (the sim's auto-succeed grammar bent one notch further), enemy 'reversion' auras (Anzu-style) are ignored. The cost is its myth-attested fidelity-1 nature: it serves whoever wears it and betrays every holder in sequence (Enlil → Anzu → Qingu → Marduk) — equipping it broadcasts your position table-wide, spawns theft events, and on your death it instantly empowers the taker. 'Whoever wears the deed IS the house — and everyone knows it.' Satisfies the loot hard rule: it is literally a remnant of belief in bureaucratized fate.
rating_notes: Influence 1 (object, not worshipped); recognition 2 = census 2 — famous inside the corpus, encyclopedia-depth outside it.

temperament: The floor's beloved first-bet concierge — every new player's chips pass under his trunk for luck before touching a table, and the house never opens a season without him.

favor_conditions: perform an offering/salute action at the run's first threshold before any combat
clear a blocking obstacle (barricade, rubble, locked route) instead of routing around it
learn a boss's win condition before dealing it any damage
sacrifice a piece of your own equipped gear to complete an objective (the broken-tusk clause)
taboos: mock a fallen contestant's failure on camera (the moon clause)
skip an available threshold offering at a run's start
let food supplies spoil or go to waste
patron_block.blessing_style: obstacles quietly resolve ahead of you — locked doors found open, traps pre-sprung; boons front-load run starts and the first attempt of anything

game_hooks: Run-start blessing economy: threshold offerings, first-attempt bonuses, pre-sprung traps. Moon-curse event: any contestant who mocks a fallen rival on camera catches the 'False Accusation' debuff, cleared by publicly tracing the Syamantaka jewel. Not a Forsaken candidate — the house's perennial favorite, never desperate.
rating_notes: FROZEN Wave-0 calibration entity — matches exactly: principal deity of a living major (>100M) = influence 5; globally recognized image short of Zeus-tier household name = recognition 4.

temperament: Ash-smeared whale who meditates through whole seasons, then bets everything — clothes included — on one hand; the house comps his suite and prays he stays asleep.

favor_conditions: voluntarily strip an equipped item or active buff before a boss and win without it
absorb a poison or burn condition meant for a teammate and survive the run (the halahala clause)
hold position without attacking for 3+ consecutive turns mid-combat, then win the encounter (the tapas clause)
finish a boss with a performance/flourish action on camera (the Tandava)
taboos: interrupt another contestant's rest or channeled action (the Kamadeva clause)
kill a non-hostile beast (Pashupati protects the wild)
boast about a victory on camera
patron_block.blessing_style: long silence, then everything at once — no drip-feed; favor accumulates unseen and detonates as one overwhelming third-eye intervention

game_hooks: Forsaken-host archetype (census forsaken_candidate; risk_appetite 5): the god who already lost everything at dice — down to his clothes (Skanda Purana) — and keeps returning to the table; hosts an all-or-nothing ash table. Third-eye = one-per-season patron ultimate that deletes a single encounter. Halahala event: a spreading poison pool one contestant must drink and hold (poison-condition endurance) to save the floor.
rating_notes: Principal deity of a living major (Shaivism, hundreds of millions) = influence 5 per Ganesha anchor; genuine global household name (Nataraja, 'the Destroyer') = recognition 5, Zeus-tier.
sources: Mahabharata Adi Parva, Astika (Ganguli tr., PD)

temperament: The only player on the floor with a lifetime winning record against the Destroyer — patient, immaculate, seated facing the door, and she brought her own banker.

favor_conditions: win a wager minigame or duel against an opponent with higher stats
retry a failed objective until it clears — 3+ attempts on the same objective, then success (the tapas clause)
shield a downed teammate until they recover
feed a starving NPC from your own supplies (the Annapurna clause)
taboos: abandon a party member mid-run
accept a rival patron's buy-out offer
refuse hospitality or food offered by an NPC
patron_block.blessing_style: steady maternal compounding — small persistent buffs that grow each consecutive run you keep faith with her; break the streak and the ledger resets

game_hooks: Casino royalty: the reigning dice champion — she beat Shiva with Lakshmi quietly staking her (Skanda Purana ch. 34) and decreed the Diwali gambling night. Diwali table modifier: all wagers prosper. Couples-table event reenacting the Akshakrida. Forsaken lens: census forsaken_candidate via the wager myths; risk 4 = an eager (never desperate) host. As her Durga battle-form she underwrites the Mahishasura boss table.
rating_notes: Principal goddess of the living major (pan-Indian worship, Shaktism) = influence 5 per Ganesha anchor; known mainly to mythology-curious audiences abroad = recognition 3 (census concurs).

symbols: gold coins pouring from an open palm

temperament: Owns the luckiest seat in the house and never sits in it twice — brag about her once and she is already gone, her sister moving into your chair.

favor_conditions: end the run with more currency than you entered with
enter the finale carrying no broken gear (she abides where things are kept well)
give a share of loot to a teammate or NPC during the run
place or light light-sources in dark map cells (the Diwali clause)
taboos: boast about winnings on camera (fortune flees the boastful)
idle through a full run phase (sloth invites her sister)
leave a debt unpaid at run's end
patron_block.blessing_style: compounding luck on loot rolls and shop odds while her conditions hold — and instant, silent departure the moment one breaks; her exit is the only warning

game_hooks: The deepest bankroll on the floor. Table aura: loot-luck multiplier while her conditions hold. Departure mechanic: a per-table boast meter — max it and she walks mid-run, hot-swapping her aura for Alakshmi's (paired-goddess rule). Her walk-out from Indra's heaven (Vishnu Purana I.9) is the myth template for patron abandonment; low fidelity is canonical (Chanchala, 'the fickle').
rating_notes: Principal deity invoked in virtually every Hindu household and business (Diwali) = influence 5 per Ganesha anchor; internationally recognizable but at Ganesha's visual tier, not Zeus's = recognition 4 (census concurs).

temperament: The slow auditor in iron-rimmed glasses — the one dealer no god can tip; he comps nothing, forgets nothing, and settles every account with interest, eventually.

favor_conditions: accept a run-start handicap (curse or debuff) offered by an event and clear the run without cleansing it (the sade-sati clause)
pay every toll, fee, or NPC debt in full at first demand
complete a full run using zero consumable items
finish a fight you started even after it turns unfavorable (no mid-combat retreat)
taboos: cheat or exploit a rigged advantage in any wager event
abandon an accepted quest or contract mid-run
patron_block.blessing_style: back-loaded justice — no boons early; every hardship endured is logged and paid out at run's end with interest, the longer the drought the bigger the settlement

game_hooks: The incorruptible house auditor: counter-NPC to Shakuni's rigged tables (invoke an audit to expose loaded dice — the one dealer who cannot be tipped, inverting the canon tipping rule for flavor). Sade-sati contract: accept a seven-phase handicap for a massive back-loaded payout. His gaze as a floor hazard: a moving line-of-sight beam that debuffs whatever it touches — it took an infant god's head off (Brahma Vaivarta Purana).
rating_notes: Dedicated living worship at scale (Shani Shingnapur, ubiquitous Saturday/navagraha observance — tens of millions) = influence 4 per Amaterasu anchor, below the >100M principal tier; encyclopedia-depth outside India = recognition 2.
sources: Brahma Vaivarta Purana Ganapati Khanda (via verified index)

temperament: The pot-bellied cage boss who has lent bankrolls to gods — one loan is still compounding at Tirupati — and whose terms outlive religions.

favor_conditions: end the run with more banked currency than you spent
repay any in-run loan or debt before its deadline, with the interest
discover a hidden treasure cache or vault room
end the run with every party member's loot intact — nothing lost, dropped, or stolen
taboos: default on a loan or stake you cannot cover
destroy or abandon loot that could have been carried out
wager the party's shared pot without consent
patron_block.blessing_style: lines of credit, not gifts — advance boons early in the run that must be repaid from winnings, at fair terms while you're good for them

game_hooks: The casino's banker and cage: in-run lines of credit with divine interest. The Venkateswara wedding-loan storyline — a supreme god in debt to him until the end of the age, with devotees paying the interest — is a ready-made season-long economy plot. Vault-heist dungeon theme: Alaka's treasuries with yaksha guards. Wave-4 note: same identity as Buddhist Vaisravana/Jambhala (syncretic_group declared for dedup).

sources: Venkatachala Mahatmya tradition / Tirumala temple lore (verified via multiple 2024-25 accounts)

temperament: Fortune's unlucky elder sister works the cold tables — nothing to her name, everything to prove, and she will go all-in on your losing streak.

favor_conditions: win an encounter while carrying an uncleansed curse
loot a fallen rival contestant's stash
enter the boss room with broken equipment and still win
provoke two NPC factions into fighting each other
taboos: cleanse a curse or purify a shrine mid-run
share food or supplies with the party (harmony repels her)
carry a purification or ward charm in inventory

game_hooks: Forsaken-host natural (§5 amendment): a goddess acknowledged by a billion-strong religion only to be ritually expelled from it — the desperate, nothing-to-lose profile; staking her own existence is fully in-character. Paired mechanic with Lakshmi: one arrives when the other leaves. Strife events: a party 'quarrel meter' that powers her boons. Collector thrill: mythic-tier rarity on the boards per canon.
rating_notes: Judgment call: living tradition acknowledges her only apotropaically (Diwali expulsion, lime-chili wards) — continuous practice but zero positive worship-wealth = influence 2, not 3; specialists only = recognition 1.

temperament: The legend the pit bosses whisper about: lost a kingdom to possessed dice, came back knowing the heart of every die on the floor.

Teaches 'Aksha-Hridaya' (the Heart of Dice, learned from Rituparna for his horse-lore) as an unlockable perk: predict/reroll wager events, count hidden totals at a glance (the vibhitaka-tree feat). Epithet template: 'the Dice-Hearted' — lose everything mid-run and win it all back within the same season. Forsaken lens: census forsaken_candidate; his ruin was engineered, his comeback a single throw.

animals: dog (Dharma in disguise, at heaven's gate)

temperament: The straightest player in the room and the easiest mark — he cannot decline a challenge, and the house knows it.

Truth-tracker mechanic: his chariot rode above the ground until his first half-lie (Drona Parva) — a visible purity meter that drops on deception. Event chains: the unrefusable challenge (kshatriya code as compulsion), the Yaksha's riddle lake with brothers' lives staked on answers, refusing paradise without his dog. temperament: The dealer you never want smiling at you — his dice answer to family loyalty, not physics, and he only plays hands he has already won.
